content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

August 6, 1990

Carole Bailey
Capistrano Unified School District
32972 Calle Perfecto
San Juan Capistrano, CA 92675

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-90-436

Dear Ms. Bailey:

John McLean, Legal Counsel with the Commission's Enforcement
Division, has asked me to respond to your June 14, 1990, letter
concerning the campaign provisions of the Political Reform Act.¹

QUESTION

Are payments made by the Capistrano Unified School District
(CUSD), as itemized in Jerome R. Thornsley's letter dated January
31, 1990, to Michael Kenney, reportable under the Act?

CONCLUSION

The payments for item 1 are not reportable under the Act.
However, the payments for items 2-9 are reportable as independent
expenditures. (Mr. Thornsley's letter is enclosed for your
convenience.)

FACTS

The Capistrano Unified School District held an election on
May 9, 1989, to form a Mello-Roos Facilities District (Measure A).
CUSD incurred expenses in relation to holding the election,
including hiring consultants, producing and distributing
informational material, and establishing phone lines for the
purpose of contacting voters.

________________

1 Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Carole Bailey
Page Two

ANALYSIS

Section 82013(b) defines a "committee" as any person or
combination of persons who directly or indirectly makes
independent expenditures totaling $1,000 in a calendar year.

An independent expenditure is defined as:

...an expenditure made by any person in connection with a
communication which expressly advocates the election or defeat of a clearly identified candidate or the
qualification, passage or defeat of a clearly identified
measure, or taken as a whole and in context, unambiguously
urges a particular result in an election but which is not
made to or at the behest of the affected candidate or
committee.

(Section 82031, emphasis added.)

Section 84200(b) requires all independent expenditure
committees to file campaign statements by July 31 for the period
ending June 30, and January 31 for the period ending December 31
if they have made independent expenditures during the six-month
period before the closing date of the statements. In addition,
depending upon the independent expenditure committee's activities,
"late independent expenditure reports" may be required.

A late independent expenditure is:

...an independent expenditure which totals in the aggregate
$1,000 or more and is made for or against any specific
candidate or measure involved in an election before the date
of the election but after the closing date of the last
campaign statement required to be filed prior to the election
by a candidate or committee participating in such election.

(Section 82036.5.)

In other words, if the independent expenditure committee made
independent expenditures which totaled $1,000 during the 16 days
before the May 9, 1989, election, a late independent expenditure
report was due within 24 hours of making the expenditures. (See
page 23 of the enclosed Information Manual E: Campaign Provisions
of the Political Reform Act for Major Donor Committees and
Independent Expenditure Committees for instructions on completing
reports.) Even though the election has passed, if late
independent expenditures were made, the committee is still
required to file the late independent expenditure reports.
However, the reports may be sent by regular first class mail and
need not be sent by guaranteed overnight

Carole Bailey
Page Three

mail, as normally required. (Pessner Advice Letter, No. A-87-263,
copy enclosed.)

In reference to Mr. Thornsley's letter, costs paid to the
Orange County Registrar of Voters to conduct the election item 1,
did not constitute an independent expenditure. However, items 2
through 9 all pertain to expenses associated with communications
which expressly advocated or, taken as a whole and in context,
unambiguously urged passage of Measure A. Although Jerome R.
Thornsley's letter of January 31, 1990, to Michael Kenney of the
Fair Political Practices Commission indicated that "no printed or
oral communication urged a "yes" vote by potential voters," the
materials provided to John G. McLean of the Commission by your
office state otherwise. As an example, the last page of Exhibit A
states:

Those who advocate a "yes" vote on Measure A are encouraged
to join in the effort to urge voters to approve this measure.

And Exhibit D, a flyer sent to Laguna Niguel and Dana Point
voters reads, in part:

There's a lot in Measure A for Laguna Niguel and Dana Point
residents. Measure A -- May 9

Therefore, since the total of independent expenditures made
in items 2 through 9 is $1,000 or more, and presumably were made
within one calendar year, CUSD qualified as an independent
expenditure committee and must file a campaign statement for each
six month period (January 1 through June 30 or July 1 through
December 31) in which independent expenditures were made, in
addition to any late independent expenditure reports that were
required. These reports must be filed with the Orange County
Registrar's office as soon as possible. A copy of the filed
reports should be forwarded to Mr. McLean.

If you have any additional questions, please contact me at
(916) 322-5662.

Sincerely,

Scott Hallabrín-
Acting General Counsel

By: Kevin S. Braaten-Moen
Political Reform Consultant

Enclosures
